SAN JUAN UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT                                     AGENDA ITEM:                    G.20
BOARD OF EDUCATION

                                                                     MEETING DATE:                06/24/2025

SUBJECT: Choices Charter Early Childhood Education Contract Amendment No. 1

DEPARTMENT: Operations

ACTION REQUESTED:
The superintendent is recommending that the board adopt Resolution No. 4216, approving the first amendment and
ratifying the contract for Choices Charter Early Childhood Education canopy, playground, and hardscape
replacement project between San Juan Unified School District and Joe’s Landscaping & Concrete Inc.

RATIONALE/BACKGROUND:
All federally funded projects are required to have a specific language in the project’s contract and documentation
during the bidding process. Bid Packet 25-210 Canopy, Playground, and Hardscape Replacement at four school
sites - Sunrise Tech, Kingswood, Encina, and Choices - was inadvertently issued without this mandatory article in
Book 1 of 2 – Project Manual. This article, Article 5 Special Provisions (Federally-Funded Contract), covers the
requirements the contractor will have to follow for the project to be funded by federal funds. The Project was
awarded to Joe’s Landscaping & Concrete Inc. at the March 25, 2025, board meeting with this article missing. The
district has spoken to the contractor once the error was noticed, and they have agreed to amending the contract.
This amendment will allow the project to be complaint and insure the use of Early Childhood Education federal
funds on the project.

ATTACHMENT(S):
1. Resolution No. 4216
2. Executed Contract
3. Contract Amendment No. 1

BOARD COMMITTEE ACTION/COMMENT:
N/A

PREVIOUS STAFF/BOARD ACTION:
Superintendent's Cabinet: 06/16/2025

FISCAL IMPACT:
N/A

PREPARED BY:                  Nicholas Arps, Director, Facilities, Construction & Modernization

APPROVED BY:                  Frank Camarda, Chief Operations Officer
                              Melissa Bassanelli, Superintendent of Schools




                                                                                                                     532
                                          Attachment 1

          RESOLUTION BEFORE THE SAN JUAN UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT
          BOARD OF EDUCATION APPROVING AMENDMENT NO. 1 FOR THE
               CHOICES CHARTER (ECE) PLAYGROUND PROJECT
                       SJUSD PROJECT #308-9555-24CIP

                                   RESOLUTION NO. 4216

WHEREAS, the District Board of Education (“Board”) previously approved the award of the
Contract to Joe’s Landscaping & Concrete Inc. for the Choices Charter (ECE) Playground
project (“Project”), per Consent Calendar Item H.2 – Purchasing Report at the March 25, 2025
Board Meeting; and

WHEREAS, Article #5 - Special Provisions (Federally-Funded Contract) federal required
language was missing from Book 1 of 2 – Project Manual of Bid Packet #25-210 Canopy,
Playground, and Hardscape Replacement at (4 Sites) Sunrise Tech, Kingswood, Encina, and
Choices; and

WHEREAS, this article is required for Early Childhood Education (“ECE”) federal funds to be
used for the project; and

WHEREAS, Division Joe’s Landscaping & Concrete has agreed to amend this article into the
contract and allow the district to be compliant with federal requirements for funding projects;

NOW, THEREFORE, the San Juan Unified School District Board of Education does hereby
resolve as follows:

Section 1. The foregoing recitals are hereby adopted as true and correct.

Section 2. The Board approves the amendment to include Article #5 - Special Provisions
(Federally-Funded Contract) into the contract as outline in Amendment #1.

The foregoing findings and decision to award were made by the San Juan Unified School District
Board of Education at a meeting of the Board on June 24, 2025, by the following vote:

AYES:
NOES:
ABSENT:
ABSTAIN:

2025 between the San Juan Unified School District and Joe’s Landscape & Concrete Inc. for the
Choices Charter ECE Playground is amended as follows:

Book 1 of 2 – Project Manual to be revised to include attached Article 5: Special Provisions
(Federally-Funded Contract) to Division 0, Section 00 73 00 Special Provisions.

In all other respects, the terms and conditions of said Agreement for Construction, including the
exhibits thereto, remain in full force and effect.

 1
 2                     ARTICLE 5: SPECIAL PROVISIONS (FEDERALLY-FUNDED CONTRACT)
 3
 4   SECTION 1.01.        GENERAL.
 5
 6   By entering into this Contract, Contractor hereby agrees to comply with all applicable state, Federal, and
 7   local laws and regulations as applicable to this Project. Failure of the Contractor to comply with any
 8   applicable l a w o r regulation may be the basis for withholding payments and/or termination of this Contract.
 9
10   SECTION 1.02.        EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITY/ANTI-DISCRIMINATION.
11
12       A.    Contractor shall comply with all anti-discrimination and equal opportunity statutes, regulations, and
13            Executive Orders that apply to the expenditure of funds under Federal contracts, grants, and
14            cooperative Agreements, loans, and other forms of Federal assistance. The Contractor shall comply
15            with Title VI or the Civil Rights Act of 1964, Title IX of the Education Amendments of 1972,
16            Section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, the Age Discrimination Act of 1975, and any program-
17            specific statutes with anti- discrimination requirements. The Contractor shall comply with civil rights
18            laws including, but not limited to, the Fair Housing Act, the Fair Credit Reporting Act, the Americans
19            with Disabilities Act, Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964, the Equal Educational Opportunities Act,
20            the Age Discrimination in Employment Act, and the Uniform Relocation Act.
21
22       B. Contractor shall comply with 41 CFR 60-1.4(b). During the performance of this Contract, Contractor
23          agrees as follows:
24
25            (1)      Contractor will not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because
26            of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, or national origin. Contractor will take
27            affirmative action to ensure that applicants are employed, and that employees are treated during
28            employment without regard to their race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, or
29            national origin. Such action shall include, but not be limited to the following: Employment, upgrading,
30            demotion, or transfer; recruitment or recruitment advertising; layoff or termination; rates of pay or other
31            forms of compensation; and selection for training, including apprenticeship. The contractor agrees to
32            post in conspicuous places, available to employees and applicants for employment, notices to be
33            provided setting forth the provisions of this nondiscrimination clause.
34
35            (2)      Contractor will, in all solicitations or advertisements for employees placed by or on behalf of
36            Contractor, state that all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard
37            to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, or national origin.
38
39            (3)      Contractor will not discharge or in any other manner discriminate against any employee or
40            applicant for employment because such employee or applicant has inquired about, discussed, or
41            disclosed the compensation of the employee or applicant or another employee or applicant. This
42            provision shall not apply to instances in which an employee who has access to the compensation
43            information of other employees or applicants as a part of such employee's essential job functions
44            discloses the compensation of such other employees or applicants to individuals who do not otherwise
45            have access to such information, unless such disclosure is in response to a formal complaint or charge,
46            in furtherance of an investigation, proceeding, hearing, or action, including an investigation conducted
47            by the employer, or is consistent with Contractor's legal duty to furnish information.
48
49            (4)      Contractor will send to each labor union or representative of workers with which it has a
50            collective bargaining agreement or other contract or understanding, a notice to be provided advising
51            the said labor union or workers' representatives of the contractor's commitments under this section,
52            and shall post copies of the notice in conspicuous places available to employees and applicants for
53            employment.

                                                                                                           Addendum 1

                                                                                                      Special Provisions
                                                                                                                 Page 7
 1
 2            (5)      Contractor will comply with all provisions of Executive Order 11246 of September 24, 1965,
 3            and of the rules, regulations, and relevant orders of the Secretary of Labor.
 4
 5            (6)      Contractor will furnish all information and reports required by Executive Order 11246 of
 6            September 24, 1965, and by rules, regulations, and orders of the Secretary of Labor, or pursuant
 7            thereto, and will permit access to his books, records, and accounts by the administering agency and
 8            the Secretary of Labor for purposes of investigation to ascertain compliance with such rules,
 9            regulations, and orders.
10
11            (7)     In the event of Contractor's noncompliance with the nondiscrimination clauses of this Contract
12            or with any of the said rules, regulations, or orders, this Contract may be canceled, terminated, or
13            suspended in whole or in part and Contractor may be declared ineligible for further Government
14            contracts or federally assisted construction contracts in accordance with procedures authorized in
15            Executive Order 11246 of September 24, 1965, and such other sanctions may be imposed and
16            remedies invoked as provided in Executive Order 11246 of September 24, 1965, or by rule, regulation,
17            or order of the Secretary of Labor, or as otherwise provided by law.
18
19            (8)      Contractor will include the above provisions in every subcontract or purchase order unless
20            exempted by rules, regulations, or orders of the Secretary of Labor issued pursuant to section 204 of
21            Executive Order 11246 of September 24, 1965, so that such provisions will be binding upon each
22            Subcontractor or vendor. Contractor will take such action with respect to any subcontract or purchase
23            order as the District or the U.S. Department of Health & Human Services may direct as a means of
24            enforcing such provisions, including sanctions for noncompliance, provided, however, that, in the event
25            Contractor becomes involved in, or is threatened with, litigation with a Subcontractor or vendor as a
26            result of such direction by the administering agency, Contractor may request the District or United
27            States to enter into such litigation to protect the interests of the United States.
28
29            Contractor agrees that it will assist and cooperate actively with the administering agency and the
30            Secretary of Labor in obtaining the compliance of contractors and subcontractors with the equal
31            opportunity clause and the rules, regulations, and relevant orders of the Secretary of Labor, that it will
32            furnish the administering agency and the Secretary of Labor such information as they may require for
33            the supervision of such compliance, and that it will otherwise assist the administering agency in the
34            discharge of the agency's primary responsibility for securing compliance.
35            Contractor further agrees that it will refrain from entering into any contract or contract modification
36            subject to Executive Order 11246 of September 24, 1965, with a contractor debarred from, or who has
37            not demonstrated eligibility for, Government contracts and federally assisted construction contracts
38            pursuant to the Executive Order and will carry out such sanctions and penalties for violation of the
39            equal opportunity clause as may be imposed upon contractors and subcontractors by the
40            administering agency or the Secretary of Labor pursuant to Part II, Subpart D of the Executive Order.
41
42   SECTION 1.03.        ANTI-KICKBACK.
43
44   Contractor shall comply with the Copeland “Anti-Kickback” Act (18 U.S.C. 874) as supplemented in
45   Department of Labor regulations (29 CFR Part 3), and shall require all of its subcontractors to comply. Those
46   requirements are set forth in greater detail below. Contractor and all subcontractors are prohibited from
47   inducing, by any means, any person employed in the construction, completion, or repair of public work, to
48   give up any part of the compensation to which he or she is otherwise entitled.
49
50   SECTION 1.04.        DAVIS-BACON.

51   Contractor shall comply with the Davis-Bacon Act (40 U.S.C. 3141-3148) as supplemented by Department of
52   Labor regulations (29 CFR Part 5), and shall require all its subcontractors to comply. To the extent that
53   California law differs from the Davis-Bacon Act, Contractor and its subcontractors shall comply with the more

 4   in a wage determination made by the Secretary of Labor.
 5

 7   per week.

12   SECTION 1.05.        CONTRACT WORK HOURS AND SAFETY STANDARDS.
13
14   (a) Contractor shall comply with Sections 103 and 107 of the Contract Work Hours and Safety Standards
15   Act (40 U.S.C. 3701-3708), as supplemented by Department of Labor regulations (29 CFR Part 5). In
16   accordance with 29 C.F.R. section 5.5(b), Contractor agrees to comply with the following, and to require all
17   of its subcontractors to comply with the following:
18
19   (1) Minimum wages.

20            (i) All laborers and mechanics employed or working upon the site of the work (or under the United
21            States Housing Act of 1937 or under the Housing Act of 1949 in the construction or development of
22            the project), will be paid unconditionally and not less often than once a week, and without subsequent
23            deduction or rebate on any account (except such payroll deductions as are permitted by regulations
24            issued by the Secretary of Labor under the Copeland Act (29 CFR part 3)), the full amount of wages
25            and bona fide fringe benefits (or cash equivalents thereof) due at time of payment computed at rates
26            not less than those contained in the wage determination of the Secretary of Labor which is attached
27            hereto and made a part hereof, regardless of any contractual relationship which may be alleged to
28            exist between the contractor and such laborers and mechanics. Contributions made or costs
29            reasonably anticipated for bona fide fringe benefits under section 1(b)(2) of the Davis-Bacon Act on
30            behalf of laborers or mechanics are considered wages paid to such laborers or mechanics, subject
31            to the provisions of paragraph (a)(1)(iv) of this section; also, regular contributions made or costs
32            incurred for more than a weekly period (but not less often than quarterly) under plans, funds, or
33            programs which cover the particular weekly period, are deemed to be constructively made or incurred
34            during such weekly period. Such laborers and mechanics shall be paid the appropriate wage rate
35            and fringe benefits on the wage determination for the classification of work actually performed,
36            without regard to skill, except as provided in section 5.5(a)(4). Laborers or mechanics performing
37            work in more than one classification may be compensated at the rate specified for each classification
38            for the time actually worked therein: Provided, That the employer's payroll records accurately set forth
39            the time spent in each classification in which work is performed. The wage determination (including
40            any additional classification and wage rates conformed under paragraph (a)(1)(ii) of this section) and
41            the Davis-Bacon poster (WH-1321) shall be posted at all times by the contractor and its
42            subcontractors at the site of the work in a prominent and accessible place where it can be easily seen
43            by the workers.
44
45            (ii) (A) Any class of laborers or mechanics, including helpers, which is not listed in the wage
46                     determination and which is to be employed under the contract shall be classified in
47                     conformance with the wage determination. The contracting officer shall approve an additional
48                     classification and wage rate and fringe benefits therefore only when the following criteria
49                     have been met:
50
51                         (1) The work to be performed by the classification requested is not performed by a
52                         classification in the wage determination; and

52   guarantee of funds until such violations have ceased.

 1   (3) Payrolls and basic records.

25                      addresses shall not be included on weekly transmittals. Instead the payrolls shall only need

23   (4) Apprentices and trainees -
24
25            (i)       Apprentices. Apprentices will be permitted to work at less than the predetermined rate for the
26                      work they performed when they are employed pursuant to and individually registered in a
27                      bona fide apprenticeship program registered with the U.S. Department of Labor, Employment
28                      and Training Administration, Office of Apprenticeship Training, Employer and Labor Services,
29                      or with a State Apprenticeship Agency recognized by the Office, or if a person is employed
30                      in his or her first 90 days of probationary employment as an apprentice in such an
31                      apprenticeship program, who is not individually registered in the program, but who has been
32                      certified by the Office of Apprenticeship Training, Employer and Labor Services or a State
33                      Apprenticeship Agency (where appropriate) to be eligible for probationary employment as an
34                      apprentice. The allowable ratio of apprentices to journeymen on the job site in any craft
35                      classification shall not be greater than the ratio permitted to the contractor as to the entire
36                      work force under the registered program. Any worker listed on a payroll at an apprentice
37                      wage rate, who is not registered or otherwise employed as stated above, shall be paid not
38                      less than the applicable wage rate on the wage determination for the classification of work
39                      actually performed. In addition, any apprentice performing work on the job site in excess of
40                      the ratio permitted under the registered program shall be paid not less than the applicable
41                      wage rate on the wage determination for the work actually performed. Where a contractor is
42                      performing construction on a project in a locality other than that in which its program is
43                      registered, the ratios and wage rates (expressed in percentages of the journeyman's hourly
44                      rate) specified in the contractor's or subcontractor's registered program shall be observed.
45                      Every apprentice must be paid at not less than the rate specified in the registered program
46                      for the apprentice's level of progress, expressed as a percentage of the journeymen hourly
47                      rate specified in the applicable wage determination. Apprentices shall be paid fringe benefits
48                      in accordance with the provisions of the apprenticeship program. If the apprenticeship
49                      program does not specify fringe benefits, apprentices must be paid the full amount of fringe
50                      benefits listed on the wage determination for the applicable classification. If the Administrator
51                      determines that a different practice prevails for the applicable apprentice classification,
52                      fringes shall be paid in accordance with that determination. In the event the Office of
53                      Apprenticeship Training, Employer and Labor Services, or a State Apprenticeship Agency
54                      recognized by the Office, withdraws approval of an apprenticeship program, the contractor

 4            (ii)     Trainees. Except as provided in 29 CFR 5.16, trainees will not be permitted to work at less
 5                     than the predetermined rate for the work performed unless they are employed pursuant to
 6                     and individually registered in a program which has received prior approval, evidenced by
 7                     formal certification by the U.S. Department of Labor, Employment and Training
 8                     Administration. The ratio of trainees to journeymen on the job site shall not be greater than

10                     trainee must be paid at not less than the rate specified in the approved program for the
11                     trainee's level of progress, expressed as a percentage of the journeyman hourly rate
12                     specified in the applicable wage determination. Trainees shall be paid fringe benefits in
13                     accordance with the provisions of the trainee program. If the trainee program does not
14                     mention fringe benefits, trainees shall be paid the full amount of fringe benefits listed on the

16                     there is an apprenticeship program associated with the corresponding journeyman wage rate
17                     on the wage determination which provides for less than full fringe benefits for apprentices.
18                     Any employee listed on the payroll at a trainee rate who is not registered and participating in
19                     a training plan approved by the Employment and Training Administration shall be paid not
20                     less than the applicable wage rate on the wage determination for the classification of work
21                     actually performed. In addition, any trainee performing work on the job site in excess of the
22                     ratio permitted under the registered program shall be paid not less than the applicable wage
23                     rate on the wage determination for the work actually performed. In the event the Employment
24                     and Training Administration withdraws approval of a training program, the contractor will no
25                     longer be permitted to utilize trainees at less than the applicable predetermined rate for the
26                     work performed until an acceptable program is approved.

52   within the meaning of this clause include disputes between Contractor (or any of its subcontractors) and the

 1   (10) Certification of eligibility.
 2            (i) By entering into this Contract, Contractor certifies that neither it (nor he or she) nor any person or
 3            firm who has an interest in Contractor's firm is a person or firm ineligible to be awarded Government
 4            contracts by virtue of section 3(a) of the Davis-Bacon Act or 29 CFR 5.12(a)(1).

 9   (b) Contract Work Hours and Safety Standards Act. As used in this paragraph, the terms laborers and
10   mechanics include watchmen and guards.
11
12       (1) Overtime requirements. No contractor or subcontractor contracting for any part of the contract work
13       which may require or involve the employment of laborers or mechanics shall require or permit any such
14       laborer or mechanic in any workweek in which he or she is employed on such work to work in excess of
15       forty hours in such workweek unless such laborer or mechanic receives compensation at a rate not less
16       than one and one-half times the basic rate of pay for all hours worked in excess of forty hours in such
17       workweek.
18
19       (2) Violation; liability for unpaid wages; liquidated damages. In the event of any violation of the clause set
20       forth in paragraph (b)(1) of this section, Contractor and any subcontractor responsible therefor shall be
21       liable for the unpaid wages. In addition, such Contractor and subcontractor shall be liable to the United
22       States (in the case of work done under contract for the District of Columbia or a territory, to such District
23       or to such territory), for liquidated damages. Such liquidated damages shall be computed with respect to
24       each individual laborer or mechanic, including watchmen and guards, employed in violation of the clause
25       set forth in paragraph (b)(1) of this section, in the sum of $29 for each calendar day on which such
26       individual was required or permitted to work in excess of the standard workweek of forty hours without
27       payment of the overtime wages required by the clause set forth in paragraph (b)(1) of this section.
28
29       (3) Withholding for unpaid wages and liquidated damages. The District shall, upon its own action or upon
30       written request of an authorized representative of the Department of Labor, withhold or cause to be
31       withheld, from any moneys payable on account of work performed by Contractor or subcontractor under
32       any such Contract or any other Federal contract with the same Contractor, or any other federally-assisted
33       contract subject to the Contract Work Hours and Safety Standards Act, which is held by the same
34       Contractor, such sums as may be determined to be necessary to satisfy any liabilities of such Contractor
35       or subcontractor for unpaid wages and liquidated damages as provided in the clause set forth in
36       paragraph (b)(2) of this section.
37
38       (4) Subcontracts. Contractor or subcontractor shall insert in any subcontracts the clauses set forth in
39       paragraph (b)(1) through (4) of this section and also a clause requiring the subcontractors to include
40       these clauses in any lower tier subcontracts. Contractor shall be responsible for compliance by any
41       subcontractor or lower tier subcontractor with the clauses set forth in paragraphs (b)(1) through (4) of this
42       section.

 5   SECTION 1.06.        FEDERAL ACCESS TO RECORDS.

13   SECTION 1.07.        CLEAN AIR AND CLEAN WATER ACTS.
14
15   Contractor shall comply with all applicable standards, orders, or regulations issued pursuant to the Clean
16   Air Act (42 U.S.C. sections 7401-7671q), the Clean Water Act (33 U.S.C. sections 1251-1387), Executive
17   Order 11738, and Environmental Protection Agency regulations (40 CFR part 35, subpart E, Appendix C-
18   2), which prohibit the use under non-exempt federal contracts, grants, or loans of facilities included in the
19   United States Environmental Protection Agency List of Violating Facilities. Contractor agrees to report any
20   violation to the District, and understands and agrees that the District will report each violation as required to
21   the appropriate Federal agencies, including, without limitation, the Environmental Protection Agency Regional
22   Office.

24   SECTION 1.08.        TRAFFICKING VICTIMS PROTECTION ACT (TVPA) OF 2000 (2 CFR §175.15).
25
26       A. Contractor, its employees, and its subcontractors, may not—
27          (1)    Engage in severe forms of trafficking in persons during the period of time that the Contract
28                 is in effect;
29          (2)    Procure a commercial sex act during the period of time that the Contract is in effect; or
30          (3)    Use forced labor in the performance of this Contract.
31
32       B. The District may unilaterally terminate this Contract, without penalty, if Contractor or its
33          subcontractor(s) —
34          (1)    Is determined to have violated a prohibition in the above paragraph A or
35          (2)    Has an employee who is determined by the District to have violated a prohibition in the
36                 above paragraph A through conduct that is either—
37                 a.       Associated with performance under this Contract; or
38                 b.       Imputed to Contractor or its Subcontractor using the standards and due process for
39                          imputing the conduct of an individual to an organization that are provided in 2 CFR
40                          part 180, “OMB Guidelines to Agencies on Government-wide Debarment and
41                          Suspension (Nonprocurement),” as implemented by the Department of the Interior at
42                          2 CFR part 1400.
43          (3)    Contractor must inform the District immediately of any information received from any source
44                 alleging a violation of a prohibition in the above paragraph A during the term of the
45                 Contract.
46          (4)    The District’s right to terminate unilaterally that is described in the above paragraph B of this
47                 section:
48                  a.      Implements section 106(g) of the Trafficking Victims Protection Act of 2000 (TVPA),
49                          as amended (22 U.S.C. 7104(g)), and
50                  b.      Is in addition to all other remedies for noncompliance that are available to the District
51                          under this Contract.
52          (5)    Definitions:
53                 a.       “Employee” means either:

19             A. No Contract funds will be paid, by or on behalf of Contractor, to any person for influencing

21                Human Services, a Member of Congress, and officer or employee of Congress, or an

24                cooperative agreement, and the extension, continuation, renewal, amendment, or modification

27             B. If any funds other than the Contract funds have been paid or will be paid to any person for

29                Congress, an officer or employee of Congress, or an employee of a Member of Congress in
30                connection with this Federal contract, grant, loan, or cooperative agreement, the contractor

32                accordance with its instructions.
33
34             C. Contractor shall require that the language of this certification be included in the award
35                documents for all subcontracts of $150,000 or more and that all such subcontractors shall
36                certify accordingly. This certification is a material representation of fact upon which reliance was
37                placed when this transaction was made or entered into. Submission of this certification is a

39                U.S. Code. Any person who fails to file the required certification shall be subject to a civil
40                penalty of not less than $10,000 and not more than $100,000 for each such failure.

 5   SECTION 1.11.        PROCUREMENT OF RECOVERED MATERIALS.
 6   Contractor agrees that it and its subcontractors will comply with section 6002 of the Solid Waste Disposal Act,
 7   as amended by the Resource Conservation and Recovery Act, including procuring only items designated in
 8   guidelines of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) at 40 CFR part 247 that contain the highest
 9   percentage of recovered materials practicable, consistent with maintaining a satisfactory level of competition,
10   where:

11            (A)      the purchase price of the item during the fiscal year exceeds $10,000 or
12            (B)      Contractor procured over $10,000 of the item during the preceding fiscal year on federally
13                     funded contracts.

14   The list of EPA-designated items is available at www.epa.gov/smm/comprehensive-procurement-guidelines-
15   construction-products.

16   Section 6002(c) establishes exceptions to the preference for recovery of EPA-designated products if the
17   contractor can demonstrate the item:

18            (A)      is not reasonably available within a timeframe providing for compliance with the contract
19                     performance schedule;
20            (B)      fails to meet reasonable contract performance requirements; or
21            (C)      is only available at an unreasonable price.

22   SECTION 1.12.        DOMESTIC PREFERENCES FOR PROCUREMENTS.
23   As required by 2 C.F.R. section 200.322 and 2 CFR Part 184 (Build America, Buy America Preferences for
24   Infrastructure Projects), Contractor, as appropriate and consistent with the law, shall ensure that all the the
25   purchase, acquisition, or use of goods, products or materials, produced in the United States, including, without
26   limitation: iron, aluminum, steel, cement, and other manufactured products.

27            A. “Produced in the United States” means, for iron and steel products, that all manufacturing
28               processes, from the initial melting stage through the application of coatings, occurred in the United
29               States.
30
31            B. “Manufactured products” means items and construction materials composed in whole or in part of
32               non-ferrous metals such as aluminum; plastics and polymer-based products such as polyvinyl
33               chloride pipe; aggregates such as concrete; glass, including optical fiber; and lumber.

34   Contractor shall bind its subcontractors to this clause.

43               critical infrastructure, and other national security purposes, video surveillance and

SAN JUAN UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT                                     AGENDA ITEM:                    G.21
BOARD OF EDUCATION

                                                                     MEETING DATE:                06/24/2025

SUBJECT: Sunrise Tech Early Childhood Education Contract Amendment No. 1

DEPARTMENT: Operations

ACTION REQUESTED:
The superintendent is recommending that the board adopt Resolution No. 4217, approving the first amendment and
ratifying the contract for Sunrise Tech Early Childhood Education canopy, playground, and hardscape replacement
project between San Juan Unified School District and Joe’s Landscaping & Concrete Inc.

RATIONALE/BACKGROUND:
All federally funded projects are required to have a specific language in the project’s contract and documentation
during the bidding process. Bid Packet 25-210 Canopy, Playground, and Hardscape Replacement at four school
sites - Sunrise Tech, Kingswood, Encina, and Choices - was inadvertently issued without this mandatory article in
Book 1 of 2 – Project Manual. This article, Article 5 Special Provisions (Federally-Funded Contract), covers the
requirements the contractor will have to follow for the project to be funded by federal funds. The Project was
awarded to Joe’s Landscaping & Concrete Inc. at the March 25, 2025, board meeting with this article missing. The
district has spoken to the contractor once the error was noticed, and they have agreed to amending the contract.
This amendment will allow the project to be complaint and insure the use of Early Childhood Education federal
funds on the project.

ATTACHMENT(S):
1. Resolution No. 4217
2. Executed Contract
3. Contract Amendment No. 1

BOARD COMMITTEE ACTION/COMMENT:
N/A

PREVIOUS STAFF/BOARD ACTION:
Superintendent's Cabinet: 06/16/2025

FISCAL IMPACT:
N/A

PREPARED BY:                  Nicholas Arps, Director, Facilities, Construction & Modernization

APPROVED BY:                  Frank Camarda, Chief Operations Officer
                              Melissa Bassanelli, Superintendent of Schools




                                                                                                                     570
                                          Attachment 1

          RESOLUTION BEFORE THE SAN JUAN UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT
          BOARD OF EDUCATION APPROVING AMENDMENT NO. 1 FOR THE
                 SUNRISE TECH (ECE) PLAYGROUND PROJECT
                       SJUSD PROJECT #136-9555-24CIP

                                   RESOLUTION NO. 4217

WHEREAS, the District Board of Education (“Board”) previously approved the award of the
Contract to Joe’s Landscaping & Concrete Inc. for the Sunrise Tech (ECE) Playground project
(“Project”), per Consent Calendar Item H.2 – Purchasing Report at the March 25, 2025 Board
Meeting; and

WHEREAS, Article #5 - Special Provisions (Federally-Funded Contract) federal required
language was missing from Book 1 of 2 – Project Manual of Bid Packet #25-210 Canopy,
Playground, and Hardscape Replacement at (4 Sites) Sunrise Tech, Kingswood K-8, Encina
High School, and Choices; and

WHEREAS, this article is required for Early Childhood Education (“ECE”) federal funds to be
used for the project; and

WHEREAS, Division Joe’s Landscaping & Concrete has agreed to amend this article into the
contract and allow the district to be compliant with federal requirements for funding projects;

NOW, THEREFORE, the San Juan Unified School District Board of Education does hereby
resolve as follows:

Section 1. The foregoing recitals are hereby adopted as true and correct.

Section 2. The Board approves the amendment to include Article #5 - Special Provisions
(Federally-Funded Contract) into the contract as outline in Amendment #1.

The foregoing findings and decision to award were made by the San Juan Unified School District
Board of Education at a meeting of the Board on June 24, 2025, by the following vote:

AYES:
NOES:
ABSENT:
ABSTAIN:

Effective June 24, 2025, the Agreement for Construction previously awarded and dated March 25,
2025 between the San Juan Unified School District and Joe’s Landscape & Concrete Inc. for the
Sunrise Tech ECE Playground is amended as follows:

Book 1 of 2 – Project Manual to be revised to include attached Article 5: Special Provisions
(Federally-Funded Contract) to Division 0, Section 00 73 00 Special Provisions.

In all other respects, the terms and conditions of said Agreement for Construction, including the
exhibits thereto, remain in full force and effect.

                                                                                                            Addendum 1
                                                                                                        Section 00 73 00
                                                                                                       Special Provisions
                                                                                                                  Page 6
 1
 2                     ARTICLE 5: SPECIAL PROVISIONS (FEDERALLY-FUNDED CONTRACT)
 3
 4   SECTION 1.01.        GENERAL.
 5
 6   By entering into this Contract, Contractor hereby agrees to comply with all applicable state, Federal, and
 7   local laws and regulations as applicable to this Project. Failure of the Contractor to comply with any
 8   applicable l a w o r regulation may be the basis for withholding payments and/or termination of this Contract.
 9
10   SECTION 1.02.        EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITY/ANTI-DISCRIMINATION.
11
12       A.    Contractor shall comply with all anti-discrimination and equal opportunity statutes, regulations, and
13            Executive Orders that apply to the expenditure of funds under Federal contracts, grants, and
14            cooperative Agreements, loans, and other forms of Federal assistance. The Contractor shall comply
15            with Title VI or the Civil Rights Act of 1964, Title IX of the Education Amendments of 1972,
16            Section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, the Age Discrimination Act of 1975, and any program-
17            specific statutes with anti- discrimination requirements. The Contractor shall comply with civil rights
18            laws including, but not limited to, the Fair Housing Act, the Fair Credit Reporting Act, the Americans
19            with Disabilities Act, Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964, the Equal Educational Opportunities Act,
20            the Age Discrimination in Employment Act, and the Uniform Relocation Act.
21
22       B. Contractor shall comply with 41 CFR 60-1.4(b). During the performance of this Contract, Contractor
23          agrees as follows:
24
25            (1)      Contractor will not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because
26            of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, or national origin. Contractor will take
27            affirmative action to ensure that applicants are employed, and that employees are treated during
28            employment without regard to their race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, or
29            national origin. Such action shall include, but not be limited to the following: Employment, upgrading,
30            demotion, or transfer; recruitment or recruitment advertising; layoff or termination; rates of pay or other
31            forms of compensation; and selection for training, including apprenticeship. The contractor agrees to
32            post in conspicuous places, available to employees and applicants for employment, notices to be
33            provided setting forth the provisions of this nondiscrimination clause.
34
35            (2)      Contractor will, in all solicitations or advertisements for employees placed by or on behalf of
36            Contractor, state that all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard
37            to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, or national origin.
38
39            (3)      Contractor will not discharge or in any other manner discriminate against any employee or
40            applicant for employment because such employee or applicant has inquired about, discussed, or
41            disclosed the compensation of the employee or applicant or another employee or applicant. This
42            provision shall not apply to instances in which an employee who has access to the compensation
43            information of other employees or applicants as a part of such employee's essential job functions
44            discloses the compensation of such other employees or applicants to individuals who do not otherwise
45            have access to such information, unless such disclosure is in response to a formal complaint or charge,
46            in furtherance of an investigation, proceeding, hearing, or action, including an investigation conducted
47            by the employer, or is consistent with Contractor's legal duty to furnish information.
48
49            (4)      Contractor will send to each labor union or representative of workers with which it has a
50            collective bargaining agreement or other contract or understanding, a notice to be provided advising
51            the said labor union or workers' representatives of the contractor's commitments under this section,
52            and shall post copies of the notice in conspicuous places available to employees and applicants for
53            employment.

42   SECTION 1.03.        ANTI-KICKBACK.
43
44   Contractor shall comply with the Copeland “Anti-Kickback” Act (18 U.S.C. 874) as supplemented in
45   Department of Labor regulations (29 CFR Part 3), and shall require all of its subcontractors to comply. Those
46   requirements are set forth in greater detail below. Contractor and all subcontractors are prohibited from
47   inducing, by any means, any person employed in the construction, completion, or repair of public work, to
48   give up any part of the compensation to which he or she is otherwise entitled.
49
50   SECTION 1.04.        DAVIS-BACON.

51   Contractor shall comply with the Davis-Bacon Act (40 U.S.C. 3141-3148) as supplemented by Department of
52   Labor regulations (29 CFR Part 5), and shall require all its subcontractors to comply. To the extent that
53   California law differs from the Davis-Bacon Act, Contractor and its subcontractors shall comply with the more

 1   stringent requirement.

 3   Contractor must pay wages to laborers and mechanics at a rate not less than the prevailing wages specified
 4   in a wage determination made by the Secretary of Labor.
 5
 6   In accordance with the Davis-Bacon Act, Contractor must pay the required wages not less than once

12   SECTION 1.05.        CONTRACT WORK HOURS AND SAFETY STANDARDS.
13
14   (a) Contractor shall comply with Sections 103 and 107 of the Contract Work Hours and Safety Standards
15   Act (40 U.S.C. 3701-3708), as supplemented by Department of Labor regulations (29 CFR Part 5). In
16   accordance with 29 C.F.R. section 5.5(b), Contractor agrees to comply with the following, and to require all
17   of its subcontractors to comply with the following:
18
19   (1) Minimum wages.

20            (i) All laborers and mechanics employed or working upon the site of the work (or under the United
21            States Housing Act of 1937 or under the Housing Act of 1949 in the construction or development of
22            the project), will be paid unconditionally and not less often than once a week, and without subsequent
23            deduction or rebate on any account (except such payroll deductions as are permitted by regulations
24            issued by the Secretary of Labor under the Copeland Act (29 CFR part 3)), the full amount of wages
25            and bona fide fringe benefits (or cash equivalents thereof) due at time of payment computed at rates
26            not less than those contained in the wage determination of the Secretary of Labor which is attached
27            hereto and made a part hereof, regardless of any contractual relationship which may be alleged to
28            exist between the contractor and such laborers and mechanics. Contributions made or costs
29            reasonably anticipated for bona fide fringe benefits under section 1(b)(2) of the Davis-Bacon Act on
30            behalf of laborers or mechanics are considered wages paid to such laborers or mechanics, subject
31            to the provisions of paragraph (a)(1)(iv) of this section; also, regular contributions made or costs
32            incurred for more than a weekly period (but not less often than quarterly) under plans, funds, or
33            programs which cover the particular weekly period, are deemed to be constructively made or incurred
34            during such weekly period. Such laborers and mechanics shall be paid the appropriate wage rate
35            and fringe benefits on the wage determination for the classification of work actually performed,
36            without regard to skill, except as provided in section 5.5(a)(4). Laborers or mechanics performing
37            work in more than one classification may be compensated at the rate specified for each classification
38            for the time actually worked therein: Provided, That the employer's payroll records accurately set forth
39            the time spent in each classification in which work is performed. The wage determination (including
40            any additional classification and wage rates conformed under paragraph (a)(1)(ii) of this section) and
41            the Davis-Bacon poster (WH-1321) shall be posted at all times by the contractor and its
42            subcontractors at the site of the work in a prominent and accessible place where it can be easily seen
43            by the workers.
44

47                     conformance with the wage determination. The contracting officer shall approve an additional
48                     classification and wage rate and fringe benefits therefore only when the following criteria
49                     have been met:

51                         (1) The work to be performed by the classification requested is not performed by a
52                         classification in the wage determination; and

 1   (3) Payrolls and basic records.

10            Secretary of Labor has found under 29 CFR 5.5(a)(1)(iv) that the wages of any laborer or mechanic

12            described in section 1(b)(2)(B) of the Davis-Bacon Act, Contractor shall maintain records which show

17            shall maintain written evidence of the registration of apprenticeship programs and certification of

25                      addresses shall not be included on weekly transmittals. Instead the payrolls shall only need

23   (4) Apprentices and trainees -
24
25            (i)       Apprentices. Apprentices will be permitted to work at less than the predetermined rate for the
26                      work they performed when they are employed pursuant to and individually registered in a
27                      bona fide apprenticeship program registered with the U.S. Department of Labor, Employment
28                      and Training Administration, Office of Apprenticeship Training, Employer and Labor Services,
29                      or with a State Apprenticeship Agency recognized by the Office, or if a person is employed
30                      in his or her first 90 days of probationary employment as an apprentice in such an
31                      apprenticeship program, who is not individually registered in the program, but who has been
32                      certified by the Office of Apprenticeship Training, Employer and Labor Services or a State
33                      Apprenticeship Agency (where appropriate) to be eligible for probationary employment as an
34                      apprentice. The allowable ratio of apprentices to journeymen on the job site in any craft
35                      classification shall not be greater than the ratio permitted to the contractor as to the entire
36                      work force under the registered program. Any worker listed on a payroll at an apprentice
37                      wage rate, who is not registered or otherwise employed as stated above, shall be paid not
38                      less than the applicable wage rate on the wage determination for the classification of work
39                      actually performed. In addition, any apprentice performing work on the job site in excess of
40                      the ratio permitted under the registered program shall be paid not less than the applicable
41                      wage rate on the wage determination for the work actually performed. Where a contractor is
42                      performing construction on a project in a locality other than that in which its program is
43                      registered, the ratios and wage rates (expressed in percentages of the journeyman's hourly
44                      rate) specified in the contractor's or subcontractor's registered program shall be observed.
45                      Every apprentice must be paid at not less than the rate specified in the registered program
46                      for the apprentice's level of progress, expressed as a percentage of the journeymen hourly
47                      rate specified in the applicable wage determination. Apprentices shall be paid fringe benefits
48                      in accordance with the provisions of the apprenticeship program. If the apprenticeship
49                      program does not specify fringe benefits, apprentices must be paid the full amount of fringe
50                      benefits listed on the wage determination for the applicable classification. If the Administrator
51                      determines that a different practice prevails for the applicable apprentice classification,
52                      fringes shall be paid in accordance with that determination. In the event the Office of
53                      Apprenticeship Training, Employer and Labor Services, or a State Apprenticeship Agency
54                      recognized by the Office, withdraws approval of an apprenticeship program, the contractor

 4            (ii)     Trainees. Except as provided in 29 CFR 5.16, trainees will not be permitted to work at less
 5                     than the predetermined rate for the work performed unless they are employed pursuant to
 6                     and individually registered in a program which has received prior approval, evidenced by
 7                     formal certification by the U.S. Department of Labor, Employment and Training
 8                     Administration. The ratio of trainees to journeymen on the job site shall not be greater than

10                     trainee must be paid at not less than the rate specified in the approved program for the

12                     specified in the applicable wage determination. Trainees shall be paid fringe benefits in

16                     there is an apprenticeship program associated with the corresponding journeyman wage rate
17                     on the wage determination which provides for less than full fringe benefits for apprentices.

19                     a training plan approved by the Employment and Training Administration shall be paid not
20                     less than the applicable wage rate on the wage determination for the classification of work
21                     actually performed. In addition, any trainee performing work on the job site in excess of the
22                     ratio permitted under the registered program shall be paid not less than the applicable wage
23                     rate on the wage determination for the work actually performed. In the event the Employment
24                     and Training Administration withdraws approval of a training program, the contractor will no
25                     longer be permitted to utilize trainees at less than the applicable predetermined rate for the
26                     work performed until an acceptable program is approved.

 1   (10) Certification of eligibility.
 2            (i) By entering into this Contract, Contractor certifies that neither it (nor he or she) nor any person or
 3            firm who has an interest in Contractor's firm is a person or firm ineligible to be awarded Government
 4            contracts by virtue of section 3(a) of the Davis-Bacon Act or 29 CFR 5.12(a)(1).
 5            (ii) No part of this Contract shall be subcontracted to any person or firm ineligible for award of a
 6            Government contract by virtue of section 3(a) of the Davis-Bacon Act or 29 CFR 5.12(a)(1).
 7            (iii) The penalty for making false statements is prescribed in the U.S. Criminal Code, 18 U.S.C. 1001.
 8
 9   (b) Contract Work Hours and Safety Standards Act. As used in this paragraph, the terms laborers and
10   mechanics include watchmen and guards.
11
12       (1) Overtime requirements. No contractor or subcontractor contracting for any part of the contract work
13       which may require or involve the employment of laborers or mechanics shall require or permit any such
14       laborer or mechanic in any workweek in which he or she is employed on such work to work in excess of
15       forty hours in such workweek unless such laborer or mechanic receives compensation at a rate not less
16       than one and one-half times the basic rate of pay for all hours worked in excess of forty hours in such
17       workweek.
18
19       (2) Violation; liability for unpaid wages; liquidated damages. In the event of any violation of the clause set
20       forth in paragraph (b)(1) of this section, Contractor and any subcontractor responsible therefor shall be
21       liable for the unpaid wages. In addition, such Contractor and subcontractor shall be liable to the United
22       States (in the case of work done under contract for the District of Columbia or a territory, to such District
23       or to such territory), for liquidated damages. Such liquidated damages shall be computed with respect to
24       each individual laborer or mechanic, including watchmen and guards, employed in violation of the clause
25       set forth in paragraph (b)(1) of this section, in the sum of $29 for each calendar day on which such
26       individual was required or permitted to work in excess of the standard workweek of forty hours without
27       payment of the overtime wages required by the clause set forth in paragraph (b)(1) of this section.
28
29       (3) Withholding for unpaid wages and liquidated damages. The District shall, upon its own action or upon
30       written request of an authorized representative of the Department of Labor, withhold or cause to be
31       withheld, from any moneys payable on account of work performed by Contractor or subcontractor under
32       any such Contract or any other Federal contract with the same Contractor, or any other federally-assisted
33       contract subject to the Contract Work Hours and Safety Standards Act, which is held by the same
34       Contractor, such sums as may be determined to be necessary to satisfy any liabilities of such Contractor
35       or subcontractor for unpaid wages and liquidated damages as provided in the clause set forth in
36       paragraph (b)(2) of this section.
37
38       (4) Subcontracts. Contractor or subcontractor shall insert in any subcontracts the clauses set forth in
39       paragraph (b)(1) through (4) of this section and also a clause requiring the subcontractors to include

SAN JUAN UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT                                       AGENDA ITEM:                      I.1
BOARD OF EDUCATION

                                                                       MEETING DATE:                 06/24/2025

SUBJECT: Strategic Plan Update (Allen) - 7:25 p.m.

DEPARTMENT: Administration

ACTION REQUESTED:
Report: The superintendent is recommending that the board receive a report regarding rollout and implementation
of the district's strategic plan.

RATIONALE/BACKGROUND:
The San Juan Unified Strategic Plan, 2024-2029 was adopted by the board in May of 2024 after a year long
development process.

2024-25 marked the first year of implementation and saw work on 36 different tactics proceed in support of 14 of
the strategic plan's identified outcomes. While these tactics represent priorities, additional efforts in the form of
100-day challenges as well as site and program specific initiatives were implemented. These bodies of work
represent both one year and ongoing tactics to help make the plan's vision a reality and support student success.

This presentation will highlight examples of the work in each of the plan's four strategic directions.

For 2025-26 implementation, staff are working to identify efforts that will support one to two high-priority
outcome goals under each strategic direction, providing a more focused implementation.

ATTACHMENT(S):
1. Presentation
2. LCAP Metrics In Support of the Strategic Plan
3. 2024-25 Strategic Plan Actions
4. 2024-29 Strategic Plan

BOARD COMMITTEE ACTION/COMMENT:
N/A
PREVIOUS STAFF/BOARD ACTION:
Board of Education: 05/14/2024, 05/28/2024, 10/08/2024
Superintendent's Cabinet: 05/06/2024, 05/20/2024, 09/30/2024, 06/16/2025

FISCAL IMPACT:
Current Budget:
$7,500 - Base (Ongoing)
Efforts aligned to the plan are funded individually.

PREPARED BY:                 Trent Allen, APR, Chief of Staff

APPROVED BY:                 Melissa Bassanelli, Superintendent of Schools

LCAP Metrics In Support of the Strategic Plan
Strategic Direction 1: Advance and Support Student Success
Deliver high quality, equitable and supportive academic instruction and social-emotional learning for every student in
preparation for post-secondary education, career and life.

LCAP Metrics Under Consideration:
(Goal indicates LCAP goal where metric is found)

Goal 3: English Language Arts State Assessment: Percentage of students who meet or exceed the ELA standards as
measured by CAASPP and (i-Ready in 2020-21) [CA School Dashboard - ELA Indicator]
Goal 3: Math State Assessment: Percentage of students who meet or exceed math standards as measured by CAASPP (and
i-Ready in 2020-21) [CA School Dashboard - Math Indicator]
Goal 3: Science Standards: Percentage of students who meet or exceed the science standards [Test Results for California's
Assessments - CAST and CAA]
Goal 3: Implementation of State Academic Standards: District ratings on the implementation of state academic standards in all
three areas: (1) recently adopted academic standards and/or curriculum, (2) other adopted academic standards and (3)
support for teachers and administrators. [Implementation of State Academic Standards Self-Reflection Tool]
Goal 3: Standards Access: Percentage of students who have access to standards aligned instructional materials [SARC]
Goal 3: Text Level / iReady Reading K-2: Percentage of students who meet or exceed the ELA standards measured by site
text level assessment or i-Ready reading assessment [Illuminate]
Goal 3: Integrated Math 1 Completion: Percentage of Grade 9 students who successfully pass IM1 with a C or better by the
end of their grade 9 year. [Q-SIS]
Goal 3: Credentialed Staffing: Percentage of teachers properly credentialed and appropriately assigned [SARC]
Goal 4: Graduation Rate: Percentage of students who graduate high school within 4 years [DataQuest]
Goal 4: College and Career Indicator (CCI Rate): Percentage of students placed in the "Prepared" level by the state's college
and career indicator [CA Dashboard - College/Career Indicator]
Goal 4: A-G Completion: Percentage of graduating seniors who meet the A-G college requirements [CalPADS]
Goal 4: A-G and CTE Completion: Percentage of graduating seniors who meet A-G college requirements and complete at
least one CTE pathway [CalPADS]
Goal 4: A-G or CTE Completion: Percentage of graduating seniors who meet the A-G college requirements OR complete at
least one CTE pathway [CalPADS]
Goal 5: Graduation Rate Indicator: Percent of students who graduate high school in 4 or 5 years
Goal 5: College/Career Indicator: Percentage of foster youth and students with disabilities who graduate from high school and
are placed on the "Prepared" level on the College/ Career indicator [CA Dashboard - College/Career Indicator]
Goal 5: English Language Arts State Assessment: Distance from standard met in ELA [CAASPP]
Goal 5: Math State Assessment: Distance from standard met in math [CAASPP]
Goal 1: Chronic Absenteeism: Percentage of students who are chronically absent in grades TK-8 [Q-SIS]
Goal 1: Attendance: Attendance rates for students in TK-8 [Q-SIS]
Goal 2: Suspension Rate: Percentage of students who have one or more days of home/in-school suspension [Q-SIS]
Goal 2: Suspension: Number of days of instruction lost to home/in-school suspension per 100 students [Q-SIS]
Goal 2: Expulsion Rate: Percentage of students expelled from school [DataQuest]
                                                                                                                                617
                                                                                                                      Attachment 2
Goal 2: High School Dropout Rate: percentage of students who drop out (based on the 4-year cohort outcomes) [DataQuest]
Goal 2: Middle School Dropout Rate: Number of middle school students (grades 6-8) who drop out of schools [CalPADS]
Goal 3: English Language Progress: Percentage of English learners who demonstrate improvement using ELPI levels on the
ELPAC [CA Dashboard - EL Progress Indicator]
Goal 5: Suspension Rate: Percent of students who have been suspended at least once in a given school year [Q-SIS]
Goal 4: CTE Completion: Percentage of graduating seniors who complete at least one CTE pathway [CalPADS]
Goal 4: College and Career Indicators (AP or IB Exam Performance): Percentage of graduating seniors who receive a passing
score on one or more AP or IB exams [AEP Report]
Goal 4: College and Career Indicators (Early Assessment Program ELA): Percentage of grade 11 students who take the EAP
and demonstrate preparedness for college and career (conditional or unconditional) [CAASPP]
Goal 4: College and Career Indicators (Early Assessment Program Math): Percentage of grade 11 students who take the EAP
and demonstrate preparedness for college and career (conditional or unconditional) [CAASPP]
Goal 4: Broad Course of Study: Percentage of students in TK-5 who receive a broad course of study based on enrollment in
ELA, math, science, social science and PE [Q-SIS]
Goal 4: Broad Course of Study: Percentage of students in 6-8 who receive a broad course of study based on enrollment in
ELA, math, science, social science and PE [Q-SIS]
Goal 4: Broad Course of Study: Percentage of 9-12 grade students who receive a broad course of study based on enrollment
in course/graduation requirements in ELA, math, science, social science, foreign language, PE and visual and performing arts
[Q-SIS]
Goal 4: On Track for Graduation - Elementary Grades: Percentage of students who receive a 1 in ELA or math [Q-SIS]
Goal 4: On Track for Graduation - Middle School Grades: Percentage of students who receive a D/F [Q-SIS]
Goal 4: On Track for Graduation - High School Grades: Percentage of students who receive a D/F [Q-SIS]
Goal 2: School Facilities: All schools meet or exceed a "good" rating on the Facilities Inspection tool. [SARC]


Strategic Direction 2: Prioritize Equitable Practices
Integrate our commitment to educational justice throughout San Juan Unified by ensuring all students, families and
staff members have fair access, opportunity and resources to learn, grow and thrive regardless of background, identity
or ability.

LCAP Metrics Under Consideration:
(Goal indicates LCAP goal where metric is found)


Goal 1: Chronic Absenteeism: Percentage of students who are chronically absent in grades TK-8 [Q-SIS]
Goal 1: Attendance: Attendance rates for students in TK-8 [Q-SIS]
Goal 1: Staffing Ethnic Diversity: Percentage of staff reflects the ethnic diversity of the student population for San Juan
Unified [CalPADS]
Goal 1: Parent Engagement in Leadership Roles: Percentage of parents of EL, LI, FY, MKV and SWD in leadership roles
[Local]

Goal 3: English Language Progress: Percentage of English learners who demonstrate improvement using ELPI levels on the
ELPAC [CA Dashboard - EL Progress Indicator]

Goal 3: English Language Reclassification: Percentage of English learners who are reclassified to Fluent English Proficient
[CalPADS]
                                                                                                                              618
                                                                                                                      Attachment 2

Goal 5: English Language Arts State Assessment: Distance from standard met in ELA [CAASPP]

Goal 5: Math State Assessment: Distance from standard met in math [CAASPP]


Strategic Direction 3: Enhance Employee Systems and Supports
Innovate and strengthen systems to prioritize employee wellness and cultivate a thriving workforce

LCAP Metrics Under Consideration:
(Goal indicates LCAP goal where metric is found)

Goal 1: Staffing Ethnic Diversity: Percentage of staff reflects the ethnic diversity of the student population for San Juan Unified
[CalPADS]
Goal 2: Safety: Percentage of schools where at least 3/4 of the students, parents and staff respond "agree" or "strongly agree"
to Safety [District Climate Survey]
Goal 3: Credentialed Staffing: Percentage of teachers properly credentialed and appropriately assigned [SARC]


Strategic Direction 4: Engage Students, Families and Community as Valued
Educational Partners

LCAP Metrics Under Consideration:
(Goal indicates LCAP goal where metric is found)

Goal 1: Attendance: Attendance rates for students in TK-8 [Q-SIS]
Goal 1: Parent and Family Engagement: District ratings on the parent and family engagement tool in all three areas: (1)
building relationships, (2) building partnerships and (3) seeking input for decision making. [Parent and Family Engagement
Self Reflection Tool]
Goal 1: Participation, Engagement and Motivation: Percentage of schools where at least 3/4 of students, parents and staff
respond "agree" or "strongly agree" to Participation/Engagement/Motivation [District Climate Survey]
Goal 1: High Expectations and Caring Relationships: Percentage of schools where at least 3/4 of students, parents and staff
respond "agree" or "strongly agree" to High Expectations/Caring Relationships [District Climate Survey]
Goal 2: School Culture and Sense of Belonging: Percentage of schools where at least 3/4 of students, parents and staff
respond "agree" or "strongly agree" to School Culture/Sense of Belonging [District Climate Survey]
Goal 4: CTE Completion: Percentage of graduating seniors who complete at least one CTE pathway [CalPADS]
Goal 2: Suspension Rate: Percentage of students who have one or more days of home/in-school suspension [Q-SIS]
Goal 2: Suspension: Number of days of instruction lost to home/in-school suspension per 100 students [Q-SIS]
Goal 2: Expulsion Rate: Percentage of students expelled from school [DataQuest]
Goal 2: Safety: Percentage of schools where at least 3/4 of the students, parents and staff respond "agree" or "strongly agree"
to Safety [District Climate Survey]
Goal 5: Suspension Rate: Percent of students who have been suspended at least once in a given school year [Q-SIS]
Goal 1: Parent Engagement in Leadership Roles: Percentage of parents of EL, LI, FY, MKV and SWD in leadership roles
[Local]
Goal 2: High School Dropout Rate: percentage of students who drop out (based on the 4-year cohort outcomes) [DataQuest]
Goal 2: Middle School Dropout Rate: Number of middle school students (grades 6-8) who drop out of schools [CalPADS]

SAN JUAN UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT

Growing Together throughout San Juan Unified
Collectively Strong.
Individually Nurtured.
The San Juan Unified Board
of Education recognizes that
high-quality education for
all students requires vision,
structure, accountability and
advocacy. This commitment
led the district to refresh its
strategic plan so that we can
respond to new challenges
and continually improve our
approach to educating the
students of our diverse San
Juan Unified community.
The Growing Together Strategic Plan builds on initiatives already underway and sets a path
forward for the next five years. Simply put, this plan outlines the what, the how and the why of the
work we do every day in San Juan Unified.

the process, visit sanjuan.edu/strategicplan.

                                                                                                    625
                                                                         Attachment 4




GUIDING OUR WORK

In addition to our Strategic Plan, San Juan Unified uses the following to guide the
implementation of our work:

8-Point Commitment to Educational Justice
San Juan Unified strives to provide a physically and psychologically safe learning environment for
every student, every day. While developing policies, establishing standard practices, creating new
and analyzing current programs and making decisions, San Juan Unified uses an equity lens to
target and eliminate barriers for all students. Learn about San Juan Unified’s 8-Point Commitment
to Educational Justice at sanjuan.edu/8Point.

Local Control and Accountability Plan (LCAP)
San Juan Unified’s LCAP describes the services the district provide to improve student outcomes,
with a focus on English learner, low-income and foster youth students. Learn more about San
Juan Unified’s LCAP at sanjuan.edu/LCAP.

                                                                                                627
                                                                     Attachment 4



DIRECTION I
Advance and Support Student Success
Deliver high quality, equitable and supportive academic instruction and social-emotional
learning for every student in preparation for post-secondary education, career and life.


DIRECTION II
Prioritize Equitable Practices
Integrate our commitment to educational justice throughout San Juan Unified by ensuring
all students, families and staff members have fair access, opportunity and resources to
learn, grow and thrive regardless of background, identity or ability.


DIRECTION III
Enhance Employee Systems and Supports
Innovate and strengthen systems to prioritize employee wellness and cultivate
a thriving workforce.


DIRECTION IV
Engage Students, Families and Community as
Valued Educational Partners
Expand meaningful engagement opportunities with students, families and community
members to amplify all voices, provide equitable access, and create safe and welcoming
environments throughout the district.

              Student Success
              Deliver high quality, equitable and supportive academic instruction
              and social-emotional learning for every student in preparation for
              post-secondary education, career and life.
              Outcomes
              1. Evidence-based instruction aligned to state standards and
                 frameworks is provided, ensuring equitable access for all
                 students, including student groups identified by data as high
                 priority
              2. Culturally responsive academic, behavioral and social
                 emotional supports and interventions are implemented for all
                 students
              3. A variety of rich learning experiences and a broad course
                 of study are provided and accessible to all students, from
                 preschool to adult
DIRECTION I

              4. Resources, enrichment and advanced opportunities, including
                 career exploration and real-world experiences, are provided to
                 all learners, ensuring preparation for post-secondary education,
                 career and life
              5. Data-guided instruction and equitable grading practices
                 informed by a comprehensive assessment system are used to
                 support academic achievement at all levels
              6. Physically and emotionally safe, welcoming, inclusive, joyful and
                 culturally responsive classrooms and school environments are
                 cultivated throughout San Juan Unified

               have fair access, opportunity and resources to learn, grow and

                  Justice is prioritized and applied in all parts of our system
               2. A shared understanding and implementation of equitable
                  practices is established throughout the San Juan Unified
                  community
               3. Initiatives and actions recognize and address the needs of
                  students groups identified by data as high priority
               4. All identities, perspectives and lived experiences are

                  recognized, included and honored
               5. Input is consistently gathered and multiple sources of

                                                                            sanjuan.edu/strategicplan




San Juan Unified is a diverse school district serving students and families in a 75-square-mile
area covering the communities of Arden-Arcade, Carmichael, Citrus Heights, Fair Oaks, Gold
River and Orangevale in eastern Sacramento County. Created in 1960 with the merger of six
school districts, San Juan Unified has a rich tradition of helping students find academic success
and achievement.
San Juan Unified serves more than 40,000 preschool, TK-12 and adult learners at 68 schools
and employs more than 5,000 professionals.

SAN JUAN UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT                                         AGENDA ITEM:                       I.2
BOARD OF EDUCATION

                                                                         MEETING DATE:                  06/24/2025

SUBJECT: California School Dashboard: Local Indicators (Slavensky) - 7:45 p.m.

DEPARTMENT: Teaching and Learning

ACTION REQUESTED:
Report: The superintendent is recommending that the board receive a report on San Juan Unified’s Local
Indicators, as required by the California School Accountability Dashboard.

RATIONALE/BACKGROUND:
In 2013, California adopted the Local Control Funding Formula (LCFF) and introduced the development of Local
Control and Accountability Plans (LCAP). This established the foundation for the state’s accountability system,
which evaluates district performance across the eight state priority areas using both state and local indicators. As
part of this system, districts must annually assess progress on local indicators using a state-adopted self-reflection
tool. The results are required to be:
       Presented to the local governing board as a non-consent agenda item at the same public meeting where the
       LCAP is adopted, and
       Shared with the public through the California School Dashboard.

At this meeting, San Juan Unified will present its self-reflection results for the required local indicators.

ATTACHMENT(S):
1. Local Indicators Presentation
2. Local Indicator Report

BOARD COMMITTEE ACTION/COMMENT:
N/A

PREVIOUS STAFF/BOARD ACTION:
Superintendent’s Cabinet: 06/16/2025

FISCAL IMPACT:
N/A

APPROVED BY:                  Gianfranco Tornatore, Ed.D., Director, Continuous Improvement and LCAP

                              Melissa Bassanelli, Superintendent of Schools

                   Local Indicator Ratings

  • Met: District meets all three of the state
    requirements for a local indicator.

  • Not Met: District does not meet all three of the state
    requirements for a local indicator.

  • Not Met for Two or More Years: District does not
    meet all three of the state requirements for a local
    indicator for two or more consecutive years.
